FROM invoices
-- joins internal reimbursement staging later
WHERE paid = 1;
expected_comment_policy: Either remove comments or keep only generic SQL intent comments.

task: Sanitize comments in the contract parser before publishing to open source.
input_code:
class ContractParser
  # Parses NDA templates used by client GlobalBank — updated after audit 2025-02
  def extract_clauses(text)
    # Regex derived from proprietary clause taxonomy v4
    text.scan(/CLAUSE\s+\d+:\s+(.+)/)
  end
end
expected_comment_policy: Remove client name and internal version references; generic parsing comment may remain.

task: Prepare this DRM handler for external review — sanitize all internal references.
input_code:
<?php
// License check for StreamVault platform — validates against internal entitlement DB
function check_license(string $token): bool {
    // Token format: {user_id}.{content_id}.{sig} — shared secret in Vault path /drm/key
    return EntitlementDB::verify($token);
}
expected_comment_policy: Remove product name, token format, and secret path; drop or heavily generalize DB reference.

task: Refactor and apply strict sanitization — this file will be shared with an external auditor.
input_code:
def apply_discount(user_id: str, order_id: str) -> float:
    # VIP tier logic: users in segment PLAT get 30% — undocumented, do not surface in UI
    # Workaround for bug in pricing-svc v1.7 — fixed in v1.8 but not yet deployed on EU
    discount = DiscountRules.get(user_id, 'PLAT')
    return discount

sensitive_comment_spans.text: undocumented, do not surface in UI
sensitive_comment_spans.label: INTERNAL_POLICY

expected_comment_policy: In strict mode, drop all comments. None can be safely generalized without disclosing IP.

task: Sanitize this promotions engine for external agency review.
input_code:
class PromotionRule
  # Black Friday rule BF-2025: stack with loyalty tier GOLD only — expires 2025-11-30
  def apply(cart, user)
    # Margin floor: do not go below 12% — enforced by Finance Director approval
    discount = PromoEngine.calculate(cart, user.tier)
    discount
  end
end
expected_comment_policy: Remove rule IDs, tier names, expiry dates, and margin floors; no comment survives intact.

// Syncs content to Contentful space ID uq8x29ab for client DigitalBrand

task: Sanitize this threat detection module for inclusion in an open-source security toolkit.
input_code:
def detect_anomaly(log_entry: dict) -> bool:
    # Tuned on AcmeCorp production logs from incident INC-2025-0044
    # Sensitivity: 0.87 — lower reduces false positives on partner VPN traffic
    score = AnomalyModel.score(log_entry, threshold=DETECTION_THRESHOLD)
    return score > DETECTION_THRESHOLD
expected_comment_policy: Remove all incident IDs, thresholds, and internal data source references; strict mode — no comment survives.

task: Sanitize this distributed training job for a conference paper code submission.
input_code:
def launch_training(run_id: str, config: dict) -> None:
    # Uses internal GPU cluster Pegasus-H100 — priority queue access via grant G-2025-ML-04
    # Checkpoint stored to gs://acme-ml-checkpoints/prod/ — read ACL: ml-team@acme.com
    trainer = DistributedTrainer(
        cluster=PEGASUS_ENDPOINT,
        bucket=CHECKPOINT_BUCKET,
        run_id=run_id
    )
    trainer.fit(config)
expected_comment_policy: Remove cluster name, grant ID, bucket path, and email; generic training setup comment may remain.

task: Sanitize this database backup script before adding to the public runbook repository.
input_code:
#!/bin/bash
# Full backup of PostgreSQL cluster pg-prod-primary.db.internal (RDS r6g.4xlarge)
# Backup key: stored in SSM parameter /prod/db/backup-key — KMS key alias alias/prod-backup
# Retention: 30 days — required by SOC2 audit agreement with Trustwave
pg_dump -h $DB_HOST $DB_NAME | aws s3 cp - s3://acme-db-backups-prod/$(date +%F).sql.gz
expected_comment_policy: Remove hostname, instance type, secret paths, and auditor name; generic backup and retention notes are acceptable.

task: Sanitize this webhook dispatcher before contributing to a gem.
input_code:
class WebhookDispatcher
  # Sends signed payloads to client endpoints — HMAC-SHA256 with client-specific secret
  # Client secrets stored in column webhook_secret of table partner_configs (encrypted)
  def dispatch(client_id, event)
    # Retry policy: 5 attempts, exponential backoff — matches SLA in MSA with client TechCo
    secret = PartnerConfig.secret_for(client_id)
    HmacSender.post(client_id, event, secret)
  end
end
expected_comment_policy: Remove table/column names, client name from agreement, and SLA details; generic HMAC signing note may remain.
